subject: Biology
title: Competition in Nature
description: A quick win: understand Competition in Nature.

hook.text: When two species need the same resource, competition is inevitable. Only one usually wins.

buildup.text: Interspecific competition occurs between species. Intraspecific competition occurs within one.

discovery.text: Trees in a forest compete for sunlight, growing taller and wider to overshadow their neighbors.

twist.text: Sometimes competitors coexist by specializing. Warblers divide the same tree into feeding zones.

climax.text: Competition drives adaptation. Over time, it pushes species to become more specialized.

punchline.text: Compete or specialize. Nature demands one.

quiz.question: How do warblers coexist in the same tree?
options: They feed in different zones of the tree
They eat at different times of day
